taskDescription: Click on the key neuroanatomical terms related to the medulla oblongata in the text below.
textField:
The *medulla* *oblongata* is the most caudal part of the brainstem, continuous with the *spinal* cord below and the *pons* above. Its ventral surface features the *pyramids*, which contain descending *corticospinal* fibres. At the *pyramidal* *decussation*, approximately 75% of these fibres cross to form the *lateral* *corticospinal* tract.

The dorsal surface contains the *gracile* and *cuneate* tubercles, which overlie the nuclei of the same name. The nucleus *gracilis* relays fine touch and *proprioception* from the lower limb, while the nucleus *cuneatus* relays from the upper limb. Internal *arcuate* fibres from these nuclei cross the midline and ascend as the *medial* *lemniscus*.

*Wallenberg* syndrome (lateral *medullary* syndrome) results from *PICA* occlusion, causing ipsilateral *Horner's* syndrome, *cerebellar* *ataxia*, *dysphagia*, and contralateral loss of pain and temperature. The medulla houses vital *cardiovascular* and *respiratory* centres, making bilateral medullary lesions incompatible with life.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Some key terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key medullary terms.
